title: Pirc Defense: Classical
summary: Learn the dynamic Pirc Defense, where Black allows White to build a center then attacks it with piece play.
description: The Pirc is a hypermodern defense where Black lets White build a pawn center before attacking it with pieces. You'll learn why this can be dangerous for White if they don't develop carefully.
objectives: Understand the hypermodern idea: let White build, then attack with pieces.
Learn typical Pirc plans: ...Bg4, ...O-O, and central breaks like ...e5 or ...c5.
Master how to play both sides of the Pirc structure.
plans: White should develop quickly and attack before Black's pieces coordinate.
Black seeks to undermine the center and launch a kingside attack.
The Pirc is double-edged: both sides have aggressive options.
commonMistakes: Do not overextend with the pawn center. It becomes a target.
Avoid slow development. The Pirc punishes passive White play.
Do not underestimate Black's attack. It's fast and dangerous.
completionSummary: The Pirc Defense creates sharp positions where both sides have clear plans: White to attack, Black to counterattack.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.coaching: The king pawn opening. White wants to build a central pawn center.
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black prepares development
steps.instruction: Play d6, supporting the e5 square and preparing ...Nf6.
steps.coaching: This is the Pirc setup: Black avoids early central conflict to develop pieces first.

steps.title: Build the center
steps.instruction: Play d4 to claim central space.
steps.coaching: White establishes central control. Black will attack this pawn center with pieces.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn to claim space.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.instruction: The knight develops to f6, attacking e4.
steps.coaching: Standard development. The knight attacks the e4 pawn and prepares ...g6.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: Natural development. The knight supports the e4 pawn and controls d5.
steps.hint: Develop the queen's knight to c3.

steps.title: Black fianchettoes
steps.instruction: Play g6, preparing to develop the bishop to g7.
steps.coaching: This is the Pirc's main idea: fianchetto for kingside pressure against White's center.

steps.title: Develop the bishop
steps.instruction: Move Be3, developing and preparing for kingside castling.
steps.coaching: The bishop develops to a good square while keeping the king safe.

steps.title: Black fianchettoes the bishop
steps.instruction: The bishop develops to g7, targeting the center.
steps.coaching: This is the main Pirc setup: bishop on g7 gives strong diagonal pressure.

steps.title: Develop the queen
steps.instruction: Move Qd2, developing the queen and preparing castling.
steps.coaching: The queen develops to a safe square while supporting kingside development.

steps.title: Black prepares queenside play
steps.instruction: Play c6, preparing ...b5 and queenside expansion.
steps.coaching: Black prepares queenside counterplay while completing development.
